## The first app dedicated to 1980s tape-edit effects.

Visit website

iVox brings the lost art of tape multi-editing to your own computer. Inspired by the legendary editing style of The Latin Rascals, Omar Santana, and Chep Nunez — the pioneers who defined 80s freestyle, hip-hop, and the megamix era.

Subscribe

# iVoxAlternatives

Love iVox’s 1980s tape‑edit vibe? Try Logic Pro X for deep multitrack editing, Loopback for cable‑free routing, Libretto for fast, unlimited edits, DJay 2 for live chops, and ACE Studio 2.0 for AI-powered workflows.

results.title: ‎Tape It - Pro Audio Recorder App - App Store
results.content: already do that for free. So bottom line is unless you pay them a subscription the app becomes quite useless after a couple months and to be quite frank is worse than voice memos so I highly recommend finding a different alternative. Unless you have the funds to be able to just have subscriptions laying around and even then make sure you make every recording give that’s the case because the scroll feature is broken (at least for me it is). [...] Pheonix442 [...] I was really excited to find this app because I had wanted for a long time to have an alternative to voice memos that actually is usable and isn’t slow. This app worked for me for a couple of months and then stopped working. It is better than voice memos at an initial glance just from a speed and smoothness standpoint but there are multiple crucial problems which arise very shortly after using the app over time that prove that it is actually worse than voice memos. 1. The search function is unusable unless you pay a fee (the search function? Really?). 2. I can’t even scroll to find past recordings because the feature is broken and jumps around on my screen. 3. You have to pay to use stereo recording in the app. Voice memos can already do that for free. So bottom line is unless you pay
